passages.body: 从下面各题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中，选择可以填入空白处的最佳选项。

阅读下面的短文，掌握其大意，然后从短文后各题所给的A、B、C、D四个选项中，选择最佳选项。
When I was in Grade 8, my mother suggested that I help a younger student named Riley who had trouble understanding fractions (分数). At first, I wondered whether I could really be helpful, but my mother believed the experience would teach me something important as well.
The first time I visited her apartment, Riley sat at the kitchen table with her arms crossed and her head <u>____13____</u> away from me. I opened her math textbook and said, “Alright, Riley. Let’s try some problems together.” Riley looked at me impatiently and said, “Why should I learn this? I’ll probably just work at my uncle’s restaurant someday.”
Her words surprised me. In my family, education had always been considered very important. Still, I realized that simply telling her this would not <u>____14____</u> her mind. Maybe Riley needed something different from the textbook.
That evening, I thought about Riley’s words. Maybe not everything we learn is useful later, but practicing could help her in many ways. The next morning, I returned to her home. Riley was sitting quietly, holding a small notebook. I smiled <u>____15____</u> and asked her if she wanted to try a new way of learning.
![](figures/image3.png)
She nodded. I took out some flour and other materials. “Today we’ll make cookies,” I told her. “We need one and three-fourths cups of flour,” I explained. Riley looked <u>____16____</u>. “How much is three-fourths again?” I pointed to the cup and said, “It’s three parts out of four. Like cutting a pizza into parts.” Riley laughed. “Oh, like the textbook!” She started working patiently, step by step, and she built more <u>____17____</u>.
As the lessons continued, Riley started asking questions more often. I found she was beginning to <u>____18____</u> the learning way instead of avoiding it. Little by little, math no longer seemed impossible to her.
<u>____19____</u> by Riley’s progress, I decided to help other younger students in my neighborhood as well. Watching them improve gave me a deep sense of achievement. What began as a simple teaching task grew into a meaningful <u>____20____</u> far beyond the textbook, teaching me patience, creativity, and the true value of learning.

passages.name: A
passages.body:
下列每个图片代表一处科学实践活动场景，请根据人物意愿和喜好匹配最适合的图片，并将图片所对应的选项（A、B、C、D）填在相应位置上。其中一个选项为多余选项。
![](figures/image4.png)

passages.name: B
passages.body:
“Dear Daddy” Notes
My father was the gentlest and most caring dad in the world. But when we argued, he became strict and never considered that there could be another side. My words seemed to reach no one because his ears were closed. I could never win.
When my father refused to understand that I needed privacy for my phone, I felt frustrated (沮丧的) and even cried. When he noticed I was trying to explain my reason for not wearing uniforms to school in junior high, he ended our “discussion”, leaving me silently sad. Later, when he refused to let me go on my last-year class trip to Montreal, I could do nothing but run out of his room in great sadness. I couldn’t get him to listen to my side, maybe he would read it.
Hoping his eyes would be more open than his ears, I wrote a note that began “Dear Daddy” and ended with how much I respected his opinion, hated to fight with him, valued our relationship, and loved him. I put the note under his door and raced back to my room.
Minutes later, there was a knock at my door. Seeing my father’s face, I knew my note had softened his strictness and touched his heart.
Getting his agreement to go to Montreal was sweet. Even better was discovering a way to argue with him without tears, ending with a hug instead. My “Dear Daddy” notes became a way to follow up on many of our arguments. During our most heated disagreements, I knew how to get through to him, but his firm (坚定的) final word left me speechless.
Years later, my father and I still share those sweet memories about my “Dear Daddy” notes. Whenever we look back on our heated disagreements and how those little notes helped him finally understand me, we can’t help smiling. He never said he knew my little plan, but I could always tell he loved every note that helped him see my way. To this day, those notes remain one of my favorite stories between us.

passages.name: C
passages.body:
![](figures/image8.png)
Many people think that eating a fruit in summer has the same effect on your body as eating it in winter. However, new research shows a different result.
Our bodies have body cycles, which are just like our body clocks. There’s a main clock in the brain and other clocks in different body parts. These clocks work together to control different body activities. For example, at night, our bodies produce chemical substances (化学物质) that make us sleepy.
The body knows what time it is mainly through light. Since the daylight changes with the seasons, our bodies adjust (调整) accordingly. There are two types of body cycles: daily cycles and yearly cycles.
Food also influences our body clocks. The idea suggests that parts in plants can let our bodies know about the place where the plants grow. So, eating fruits and vegetables when they’re in season can help our bodies get used to the changing seasons. For example, apples are in season in autumn. Apples from this season have certain things that can help our bodies save energy and get stronger against illness as the weather turns cold.
However, these days, we can eat all kinds of fruits and vegetables at any time of the year. According to the recent study, polyphenols (多酚) which are things in fruits and vegetables are good for our health and can affect our body cycles. Different plants have different kinds of polyphenols because of their growing conditions. It is believed that eating out-of-season fruits might cause a difference between our body clock and the real environment.
Let’s take bananas as an example. Bananas grow in areas with a hot climate. In the places where they come from, they come together at a certain time of the year. If people in mild-climate (气候温和的) areas eat bananas when they’re not in season locally, it might mess up the body’s normal cycle. Our bodies may get “wrong” signals, which could possibly influence how our body processes (加工) food and our general health.
So, it’s better to eat fruits in season to keep our body cycles in line with the environment. But remember, eating any fruit is better than not eating at all.

passages.name: D
passages.body:
A large body of research has been developed in recent years to explain willpower. Most of the researchers exploring self-control do so with a clear goal in mind: How can willpower be improved? If willpower is truly easily used up, as the research suggests, what can be done to keep it strong?
Avoiding temptation (诱惑) is an effective method for keeping self-control, so please follow “out of view, out of mind” rule. One recent study, for example, found office workers are less attracted to candy in the desk drawer than that on top of their desks.
The research suggesting that we have a limited amount of self-control raises a troubling question. When we face too many temptations, are we to fail? Not necessarily. Researchers don’t believe that one’s willpower is ever completely used up. Rather, people appear to hold some willpower in reserve (储备), saved for future needs. The right drive allows us to <u>tap into</u> those reserves, allowing us to carry on even when our self-control strength has been run down. High drive might help deal with weakened willpower, at least to a point.
Willpower may also be made less easily exhausted (精疲力尽的) in the first place. Researchers compare self-control to a muscle (肌肉) that gets tired with heavy use. But there is another side to this comparison, they say. While muscles become exhausted by exercise in the short term, they are strengthened by daily exercise in the long term. Similarly, we should take regular (有规律的) practices of self-control to build willpower strength.
Studies on willpower exhaustion suggest that making a list of decisions on New Year’s Eve is the worst possible way. Being exhausted in one area can reduce willpower in other areas, so it makes more sense to focus on a single goal at a time. In other words, don’t try to give up smoking, choose a healthy diet and start a new exercise plan at the same time. Taking goals one by one is a better way. Once a good habit is in place, psychologist Baumeister says, you’ll no longer need willpower to keep the behavior. Finally, healthy habits will become routine (常规) and won’t require making decisions at all.
Many questions about the nature of self-control remain to be answered by further research. Yet it seems likely that with clear goals, good self-control and a little practice, you can train your willpower to stay strong in the face of temptation.

阅读短文，根据短文内容回答问题。
You don’t have to give up sugar or run a marathon to see real changes in your health and happiness. Instead, science supports a “start small” way. Small actions that are done regularly are what create long-lasting changes.
The secret? It’s not willpower. It’s not even goals. The best way to turn a small change into a powerful habit is not to focus on your motivation (动力), your reasons for doing it, or your long-term goals.
That is what everyone thinks, but research has shown that making small changes to the environment, the situation in which you perform the behavior, is far more powerful. Goals aren’t bad; they certainly have a purpose, but “motivation” only lasts for so long. Habits need to be two things to stick to: easy and enjoyable.
For example, do you think your goal of getting back into the jeans you wore two years ago will encourage you to hit the gym every day? Probably not. What will, then? Choosing a gym that is close to your house, preferably less than 5 kilometers away, is the most important consideration for keeping a regular exercise habit, according to our research. Even better if you are doing an activity you really enjoy or meeting a good friend there.
The best part is that once these tiny changes take root (扎根), they add up to better sleep, stronger bodies, calmer feelings and a clearer mind.
Just as small drops make a big ocean, small, regular habits can lead to great health improvements. As a result, these changes are more likely to last because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.
Start now by choosing one or two of these small habits that suit you. Once you feel confident with these, add similar habits for even greater benefits.
Instead of trying to change everything or forcing yourself to do something you hate, focus on two questions: How can I make it easy to do? And how can I make it enjoyable? That’s how you change your life, one small change at a time.

questions.stem: My little sister loves reading very much and ________ favorite book is Alice’s Adventures in Wonderland.
questions.solution: your你的；his他的；her她的；their他们的。根据“My little sister”可知，主语为女性，应用形容词性物主代词her修饰“favorite book”。

questions.stem: My uncle bought a new bike ________ me as a present on my birthday.
questions.solution: in在……里面、在某时间段；at在某个地点/时刻；on在……上面；在具体某天；for为了、给、对于。句中buy sth. for sb.“给某人买某物”是固定搭配，应填for。

questions.stem:
—Mom, ________ I use your phone to look up some information about Chinese traditional festivals?

—OK, but don’t use it for too long.
questions.solution: can能、可以；must必须；need需要；should应该。根据“Mom...I use your phone”可知，此处是孩子向妈妈请求许可，应用can表示“可以”。

questions.stem: Practicing English speeches makes Jack ________ than before.
questions.solution: 根据than可知，此处应用形容词的比较级，confident意为“自信的”，是多音节形容词，比较级为more confident。

questions.stem:
—Mike, ________ do you choose the drama club?

—Because it is a great way to express my feelings.
questions.solution: how如何；why为什么；when什么时候；where在哪里。根据答语“Because...”可知，询问的是原因，why符合语境。

questions.stem: Our class didn’t win the school basketball competition, ________ we tried our best and never gave up.
questions.solution: but但是；and和/并且；or或者；so所以。根据“Our class didn’t win...we tried our best”可知，前句表示比赛没赢，后句表示大家尽力了，前后句意存在转折关系，应填but。

questions.stem: Tina ________ to cook simple dishes when her father came back home yesterday.
questions.solution: 根据“when her father came back home yesterday”可知，此处表示过去某个具体时刻正在进行的动作，应用过去进行时，应填was learning。

questions.stem: David and his friends ________ at a library as volunteers every Saturday.
questions.solution: work工作（原形）；worked工作（过去式）；are working工作（现在进行时）；will work工作（一般将来时）。根据时间状语“every Saturday”可知，此处表示经常性、习惯性的动作，应用一般现在时。主语“David and his friends”为复数，谓语动词用原形。应填work。

questions.stem: Last month, we went to Ruijin, Jiangxi for the study trip and ________ the spirit of the Long March.
questions.solution: 根据时间状语Last month可知句子时态为一般过去时；且空处与went由and连接，构成并列谓语，时态应保持一致，都用过去式，即experienced。

questions.stem: Our life ________ greatly since the invention of AI technology.
questions.solution: changes改变（一般现在时，主语单数第三人称情况下）；changed改变（一般过去时）；has changed改变（现在完成时）；will change改变（一般将来时）。根据时间状语“since the invention of AI technology”可知，since引导的时间状语通常与现在完成时连用，表示动作从过去持续到现在或对现在造成影响。应填has changed。

questions.stem:
—Some nice paintings by our classmates ________ in the school hall last week.

—Great! Art makes our school life more colorful.
questions.solution: 根据时间状语“last week”可知，句子时态为一般过去时，主语paintings是物，与动词show之间构成被动关系，需用被动语态，主语paintings为复数，be动词需用复数过去式were，show的过去分词为shown。

questions.stem:
—Peter, do you know ________?

—Yes. It will land on the southern area of the moon.
questions.solution: 宾语从句应用陈述语序，即“引导词+主语+谓语”，排除A、D两项；根据答语“on the southern area of the moon”可知，询问的是地点，应用where引导，排除B项。故选C。

句意：我第一次去她的公寓时，Riley坐在餐桌旁，双臂交叉，头转开不看我。 
根据上下文，Riley一开始抵触学习，态度不耐烦，此处需表达“转头(不看我)”的含义，turned away from是固定搭配，意为“转过头不看”，符合语境；put away意为“收起”，took away意为“拿走”，gave away意为“赠送，泄露”，均与“抵触学习、不愿面对”的语境逻辑不符。

句意：不过，我意识到仅仅告诉她这个并不能改变她的想法。 
根据上下文，Riley对学习有抵触情绪，此处需表达“改变她的想法”的含义，change her mind是固定搭配，意为“改变主意/想法”，符合语境；read意为“阅读”，lose意为“失去”，open意为“打开”，均与“想法”搭配逻辑不符。

句意：我温柔地笑了笑，问她是否想尝试一种新的学习方式。 
根据上下文，“我”想换一种方式引导抵触学习的Riley，此处需表达“温柔地”微笑的含义，softly意为“温柔地”，符合语境；suddenly意为“突然地”，carefully意为“小心地”，nervously意为“紧张地”，均与“耐心引导”的语境不符。

句意：Riley看起来不确定。 
根据下文她的问题“How much is three-fourths again?”，说明她对分数的概念仍不清楚，此处需表达“不确定的”含义，uncertain意为“不确定的”，符合语境；hopeful意为“有希望的”，fearful意为“害怕的”，satisfied意为“满意的”，均与“对概念存疑”的语境不符。

句意：她开始耐心地、一步步地操作，并且越来越有信心。 
根据上下文，Riley通过制作饼干理解了分数，不再抵触学习，此处需表达“信心”的含义，confidence意为“信心”，符合语境；habits意为“习惯”，skills意为“技能”，trust意为“信任”，均与“学习状态改善”的语境不符。

句意：我发现她开始接受这种学习方式，而不是回避它。 
根据上下文，Riley从抵触学习到主动提问，说明她对学习的态度发生了转变，此处需表达“接受”的含义，accept意为“接受”，符合语境；refuse意为“拒绝”，question意为“质疑”，forget意为“忘记”，均与“态度转变”的语境不符。

句意：受到Riley进步的鼓舞，我决定也帮助社区里的其他低年级学生。 
根据上下文，Riley的进步激励了“我”去帮助更多人，此处需表达“受到鼓舞的”含义，Encouraged意为“受到鼓舞的”，符合语境；Bored意为“无聊的”，Moved意为“感动的”，Shocked意为“震惊的”，均与“被进步激励”的语境不符。

句意：一开始只是简单的教学任务，后来变成了一次远超课本的有意义的课程，教会了我耐心、创造力和学习的真正价值。 
根据上下文，这次教学经历让“我”也学到了很多，此处需表达“课程/教训”的含义，lesson意为“课程，教训”，符合语境；example意为“例子”，story意为“故事”，memory意为“记忆”，均与“从经历中获得成长”的语境不符。

questions.stem:
| <u>____21____</u> ![](figures/image5.png) | I love the natural world! I want to join the School Plant Watch. I’ll look at different flowers and leaves, take photos of special plants, and help our science teacher make a school plant guide. |
|---|---|
questions.solution: 根据“I want to join the School Plant Watch…and help our science teacher make a school plant guide.”可知，Liu Hao喜欢观察各种各样的花朵和树叶，想加入学校植物观察社团。对应到图片A。

questions.stem: | <u>____22____</u> ![](figures/image6.png) | I’d like to take part in the Star Watching Club. I’ll learn to watch stars, recognize well-known star groups, and write reports about the different shapes of the moon for our class newspaper. |
questions.solution: 根据“I’d like to take part in the Star Watching Club. I’ll learn to watch stars…the moon for our class newspaper.”可知，Lingling想要学习观测星星，加入观星社团。对应到图片C。

questions.stem: | <u>____23____</u> ![](figures/image7.png) | I care about our planet! I want to join the River Protection Group. I’ll check the water quality near our school, and send my information to scientists to help protect local water. |
questions.solution: 根据“I want to join the River Protection Group. I’ll check the water quality near our school, and send my information to scientists to help protect local water.”可知，Li Wei想要检测学校周边的水质，加入河流保护小组。对应到图片B。

questions.stem: How did the writer feel when her father refused her Montreal trip?
questions.solution: 根据原文第二段第3句“Later, when he refused to let me go on my last-year class trip to Montreal, I could do nothing but run out of his room in great sadness.”，作者被拒绝后很难过，选项中“Disappointed”符合语境。

questions.stem: What could the “Dear Daddy” notes help the writer do?
questions.solution: 根据原文第三段第1句“Hoping his eyes would be more open than his ears, I wrote a note that began ‘Dear Daddy’...”、第四段第2句“Seeing my father’s face, I knew my note had softened his strictness and touched his heart.”以及第六段第2句 “...how those little notes helped him finally understand me”，可知便条的核心作用是帮助父亲理解作者的想法。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the passage?

options.B: Reaching agreement isn’t important in argument.
options.C: Sweet memories often come from heated fights.
options.D: Children should consider their parents’ opinions.

questions.solution: 全文围绕作者与父亲的争执展开，作者没有在争吵中追求“赢”，而是通过写便条的方式沟通，让父亲理解自己，最终收获了更好的亲子关系，点明了“理解比输赢更重要”的核心主旨。

questions.stem: According to the passage, what can we learn about body cycles?

options.A: They have nothing to do with food.
options.B: They change as the daylight changes.
options.C: They work together because of the main clock.
options.D: They produce chemical substances to control sleep.

questions.solution: 根据第一段“However, new research shows a different result”，新研究表明在不同季节食用相同的食物对我们的身体有着不同影响，A项认为身体循环与饮食无关，不符合句意；第三段明确指出“Since the daylight changes with the seasons, our bodies adjust accordingly”，B项正确；第二段写道“There’s a main clock in the brain and other clocks in different body parts. These clocks work together to control different body activities”，说明我们不同的身体部位有着不同的时钟，它们共同控制了我们的活动，C项说它们之所以能协同工作是因为大脑中的主时钟，不符合句意；由第二段“our bodies produce chemical substances that make us sleepy”可知，身体产生的化学物质能让我们感觉到困意。D项认为可以控制睡眠，不准确，排除D项。

questions.stem: Why does the writer mention polyphenols in Paragraph 5?

options.A: To tell readers what polyphenols are in plants.
options.B: To show all fruits are good for people’s health.
options.C: To advise people to eat more fruit in cold weather.
options.D: To explain why out-of-season fruits influence body cycles.

questions.solution: 由第五段“It is believed that eating out-of-season fruits might cause a difference between our body clock and the real environment”，可知作者介绍多酚主要是为了解释为什么非季节水果会影响我们的身体循环。D项符合句意。

questions.stem: What is the passage mainly about?

options.A: The differences between summer fruits and winter fruits.
options.B: The ways to keep our body clocks working properly all year round.
options.C: The relationship between eating seasonal fruits and our body cycles.
options.D: The reasons why our bodies need different chemicals in different seasons.

questions.solution: 本文主要介绍了食用应季水果和身体循环之间的关联，C项符合文章主旨。

questions.stem: From the studies, we can learn that ________.

options.A: daily activities can keep people’s willpower active
options.B: willpower is not easily completely used up in daily life
options.C: strong wishes are the key to solving willpower problems
options.D: people can improve their willpower by controlling themselves less

questions.solution: 第三段明确指出：“Researchers don’t believe that one’s willpower is ever completely used up. Rather, ... in reserve.” 说明意志力在日常生活中不会完全耗尽，总有储备。  A项“日常活动保持意志力活跃”文中未直接支持；C项“强烈愿望是关键”文中提到“high drive”但非关键；D项“通过减少自我控制来改善”与原文相反（需多练习）。

questions.stem: What does the phrase “tap into” in Paragraph 3 most probably mean?
questions.solution: 前文说人们有储备的意志力“hold some willpower in reserve”，后文说“让我们能继续下去(allowing us to carry on)”，所以此处应是利用那些储备。选项D符合。

questions.stem: To form a good habit, which of the following does the writer prefer?

options.A: Focusing on one goal before moving on to another.
options.B: Preparing a long list of decisions on New Year’s Eve.
options.C: Trying different plans to develop healthy daily habits.
options.D: Carrying out several lifestyle changes at the same time.

questions.solution: 第五段明确说：“making a list of decisions ... is the worst possible way... it makes more sense to focus on a single goal at a time... Taking goals one by one is a better way.” 因此作者偏好一次专注一个目标，完成后再进行下一个。  B、C、D均与原文相反。

questions.stem: What is the writer’s main purpose in writing this passage?

options.A: To offer advice on how to improve and keep willpower.
options.B: To explain why people’s willpower becomes weak easily.
options.C: To show how to avoid different kinds of daily temptations.
options.D: To discuss the problems caused by poor self-control in life.

questions.solution: 全文开篇问“How can willpower be improved?” 随后给出各种方法：避免诱惑、利用储备、像肌肉一样锻炼、一次一个目标等。结尾总结“you can train your willpower”。整体目的是提供改善和保持意志力的建议。选项B解释为何变弱（只是部分内容），选项C仅提避免诱惑（片面），D讨论自控差导致的问题（非重点）。

questions.answer: Small actions that are done regularly.
questions.solution:
Small actions that are done regularly.

详解：根据原文第一段第3句“Small actions that are done regularly are what create long-lasting changes.”，可知规律进行的小行动能带来长期改变，答案是原文直接信息。

questions.answer: Habits that are easy and enjoyable.
questions.solution:
Habits that are easy and enjoyable.

详解：根据原文第三段第3句“Habits need to be two things to stick to: easy and enjoyable.”，可知容易坚持的习惯需要满足“简单”和“令人愉快”两个条件，答案是原文直接信息。

questions.answer: Because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.
questions.solution:
Because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.

详解：根据原文第六段第2句“As a result, these changes are more likely to last because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.”，可知这些改变之所以可能持续，是因为它们来自反复进行的相同行为，而非短时间的努力，答案是原文直接信息。

questions.stem:
What small habit do you have for better health or daily life, and why does it work for you? (Please give two reasons.)

____________________________________________________
questions.solution:
I have a habit of taking a 10-minute walk every night. First, it is easy to stick to because it doesn’t take much time and I can walk in the park near my home. Second, I enjoy listening to light music while walking, which makes the activity relaxing and fun for me.

详解：本题是开放性试题，答案不唯一，但观点需基于文中提到的习惯特点，如“简单易做”、“令人愉快”、“规律进行”等，结合自身生活经历作答即可。

<u>I have some suggestions.</u>   First, we can set up sports clubs like basketball and swimming. They help us keep fit and learn teamwork. Second, an art club is a good idea. We can draw and make crafts, making school life more colorful. Third, as many students love music, we can set up music clubs. We can enjoy music and talk about our favourite music as well. These activities are not only fun but also useful for our growth.   <u>I hope my suggestions will be put into practice.</u>

详解：［第一步：审题立意］ 
确定文体：介绍性说明文，用一般现在时为主 
明确要点：校园课后活动提议及理由
确定人称：第一人称（I/we）
注意事项：运用所给提示词语
［第二步：构思布局］
三段式结构： 
开头段：总起句，引出建议
主体段：分别介绍校园课后活动提议及理由
结尾段：总结与倡议
［第三步：要点展开］
要点一：校园课后活动提议及理由
运动俱乐部：set up sports clubs like basketball and swimming/keep fit and learn teamwork/healthy
美术俱乐部：an art club is a good idea/draw and make crafts/making school life more colorful/skills
音乐俱乐部：as many students love music/music clubs/enjoy music/talk about our favourite music as well /sing songs
要点二：总结
总结：not only fun but also useful for our growth/meaningful/lively
题目②

[二选一备选题目，与上一题任选其一作答；本题不重复计分]
<u>I’d like to share my experience of improving myself.</u>       Last term, I was worried about my poor English. So I decided to work harder. I asked my English teacher for help. He advised me to listen to the English audio as much as possible. What’s more, I read English every morning and kept trying to speak more. Finally, I got a high score in the exam.       This success has made me confident. I learn that if we keep trying, we can become better. I believe I will do even better in the future.

详解：［第一步：审题立意］ 
确定文体：介绍性记叙文，用一般过去时为主 
明确要点：自己进步的经历、从中学到了什么
确定人称：第一人称（I/my）
注意事项：不得透露真实姓名和学校名称
［第二步：构思布局］
三段式结构： 
开头段：总起句，引出对自己经历的介绍
主体段：分别介绍自己进步的经历
结尾段：总结自己从中学到了什么，升华主题
［第三步：要点展开］
要点一：自己进步的经历
起因：was worried about my poor English/in bad condition
经过：decided to work harder/asked my English teacher for help/advised me to listen to the English audio/as much as possible/What’s more/read English every morning/kept trying to speak more
结果：Finally/got a high score in the exam/get better
要点二： 从中学到了什么
收获：made me confident/learned that if we keep trying/become better/do even better in the future/nothing is impossible

answers.solution: your你的；his他的；her她的；their他们的。根据“My little sister”可知，主语为女性，应用形容词性物主代词her修饰“favorite book”。
in在……里面、在某时间段；at在某个地点/时刻；on在……上面；在具体某天；for为了、给、对于。句中buy sth. for sb.“给某人买某物”是固定搭配，应填for。
can能、可以；must必须；need需要；should应该。根据“Mom...I use your phone”可知，此处是孩子向妈妈请求许可，应用can表示“可以”。
根据than可知，此处应用形容词的比较级，confident意为“自信的”，是多音节形容词，比较级为more confident。
how如何；why为什么；when什么时候；where在哪里。根据答语“Because...”可知，询问的是原因，why符合语境。
but但是；and和/并且；or或者；so所以。根据“Our class didn’t win...we tried our best”可知，前句表示比赛没赢，后句表示大家尽力了，前后句意存在转折关系，应填but。
根据“when her father came back home yesterday”可知，此处表示过去某个具体时刻正在进行的动作，应用过去进行时，应填was learning。
work工作（原形）；worked工作（过去式）；are working工作（现在进行时）；will work工作（一般将来时）。根据时间状语“every Saturday”可知，此处表示经常性、习惯性的动作，应用一般现在时。主语“David and his friends”为复数，谓语动词用原形。应填work。
根据时间状语Last month可知句子时态为一般过去时；且空处与went由and连接，构成并列谓语，时态应保持一致，都用过去式，即experienced。
changes改变（一般现在时，主语单数第三人称情况下）；changed改变（一般过去时）；has changed改变（现在完成时）；will change改变（一般将来时）。根据时间状语“since the invention of AI technology”可知，since引导的时间状语通常与现在完成时连用，表示动作从过去持续到现在或对现在造成影响。应填has changed。
根据时间状语“last week”可知，句子时态为一般过去时，主语paintings是物，与动词show之间构成被动关系，需用被动语态，主语paintings为复数，be动词需用复数过去式were，show的过去分词为shown。
宾语从句应用陈述语序，即“引导词+主语+谓语”，排除A、D两项；根据答语“on the southern area of the moon”可知，询问的是地点，应用where引导，排除B项。故选C。

句意：我第一次去她的公寓时，Riley坐在餐桌旁，双臂交叉，头转开不看我。 
根据上下文，Riley一开始抵触学习，态度不耐烦，此处需表达“转头(不看我)”的含义，turned away from是固定搭配，意为“转过头不看”，符合语境；put away意为“收起”，took away意为“拿走”，gave away意为“赠送，泄露”，均与“抵触学习、不愿面对”的语境逻辑不符。

句意：不过，我意识到仅仅告诉她这个并不能改变她的想法。 
根据上下文，Riley对学习有抵触情绪，此处需表达“改变她的想法”的含义，change her mind是固定搭配，意为“改变主意/想法”，符合语境；read意为“阅读”，lose意为“失去”，open意为“打开”，均与“想法”搭配逻辑不符。

句意：我温柔地笑了笑，问她是否想尝试一种新的学习方式。 
根据上下文，“我”想换一种方式引导抵触学习的Riley，此处需表达“温柔地”微笑的含义，softly意为“温柔地”，符合语境；suddenly意为“突然地”，carefully意为“小心地”，nervously意为“紧张地”，均与“耐心引导”的语境不符。

句意：Riley看起来不确定。 
根据下文她的问题“How much is three-fourths again?”，说明她对分数的概念仍不清楚，此处需表达“不确定的”含义，uncertain意为“不确定的”，符合语境；hopeful意为“有希望的”，fearful意为“害怕的”，satisfied意为“满意的”，均与“对概念存疑”的语境不符。

句意：她开始耐心地、一步步地操作，并且越来越有信心。 
根据上下文，Riley通过制作饼干理解了分数，不再抵触学习，此处需表达“信心”的含义，confidence意为“信心”，符合语境；habits意为“习惯”，skills意为“技能”，trust意为“信任”，均与“学习状态改善”的语境不符。

句意：我发现她开始接受这种学习方式，而不是回避它。 
根据上下文，Riley从抵触学习到主动提问，说明她对学习的态度发生了转变，此处需表达“接受”的含义，accept意为“接受”，符合语境；refuse意为“拒绝”，question意为“质疑”，forget意为“忘记”，均与“态度转变”的语境不符。

句意：受到Riley进步的鼓舞，我决定也帮助社区里的其他低年级学生。 
根据上下文，Riley的进步激励了“我”去帮助更多人，此处需表达“受到鼓舞的”含义，Encouraged意为“受到鼓舞的”，符合语境；Bored意为“无聊的”，Moved意为“感动的”，Shocked意为“震惊的”，均与“被进步激励”的语境不符。

句意：一开始只是简单的教学任务，后来变成了一次远超课本的有意义的课程，教会了我耐心、创造力和学习的真正价值。 
根据上下文，这次教学经历让“我”也学到了很多，此处需表达“课程/教训”的含义，lesson意为“课程，教训”，符合语境；example意为“例子”，story意为“故事”，memory意为“记忆”，均与“从经历中获得成长”的语境不符。
根据“I want to join the School Plant Watch…and help our science teacher make a school plant guide.”可知，Liu Hao喜欢观察各种各样的花朵和树叶，想加入学校植物观察社团。对应到图片A。
根据“I’d like to take part in the Star Watching Club. I’ll learn to watch stars…the moon for our class newspaper.”可知，Lingling想要学习观测星星，加入观星社团。对应到图片C。
根据“I want to join the River Protection Group. I’ll check the water quality near our school, and send my information to scientists to help protect local water.”可知，Li Wei想要检测学校周边的水质，加入河流保护小组。对应到图片B。
根据原文第二段第3句“Later, when he refused to let me go on my last-year class trip to Montreal, I could do nothing but run out of his room in great sadness.”，作者被拒绝后很难过，选项中“Disappointed”符合语境。

根据原文第三段第1句“Hoping his eyes would be more open than his ears, I wrote a note that began ‘Dear Daddy’...”、第四段第2句“Seeing my father’s face, I knew my note had softened his strictness and touched his heart.”以及第六段第2句 “...how those little notes helped him finally understand me”，可知便条的核心作用是帮助父亲理解作者的想法。
全文围绕作者与父亲的争执展开，作者没有在争吵中追求“赢”，而是通过写便条的方式沟通，让父亲理解自己，最终收获了更好的亲子关系，点明了“理解比输赢更重要”的核心主旨。

根据第一段“However, new research shows a different result”，新研究表明在不同季节食用相同的食物对我们的身体有着不同影响，A项认为身体循环与饮食无关，不符合句意；第三段明确指出“Since the daylight changes with the seasons, our bodies adjust accordingly”，B项正确；第二段写道“There’s a main clock in the brain and other clocks in different body parts. These clocks work together to control different body activities”，说明我们不同的身体部位有着不同的时钟，它们共同控制了我们的活动，C项说它们之所以能协同工作是因为大脑中的主时钟，不符合句意；由第二段“our bodies produce chemical substances that make us sleepy”可知，身体产生的化学物质能让我们感觉到困意。D项认为可以控制睡眠，不准确，排除D项。
由第五段“It is believed that eating out-of-season fruits might cause a difference between our body clock and the real environment”，可知作者介绍多酚主要是为了解释为什么非季节水果会影响我们的身体循环。D项符合句意。
本文主要介绍了食用应季水果和身体循环之间的关联，C项符合文章主旨。

第三段明确指出：“Researchers don’t believe that one’s willpower is ever completely used up. Rather, ... in reserve.” 说明意志力在日常生活中不会完全耗尽，总有储备。  A项“日常活动保持意志力活跃”文中未直接支持；C项“强烈愿望是关键”文中提到“high drive”但非关键；D项“通过减少自我控制来改善”与原文相反（需多练习）。
前文说人们有储备的意志力“hold some willpower in reserve”，后文说“让我们能继续下去(allowing us to carry on)”，所以此处应是利用那些储备。选项D符合。

第五段明确说：“making a list of decisions ... is the worst possible way... it makes more sense to focus on a single goal at a time... Taking goals one by one is a better way.” 因此作者偏好一次专注一个目标，完成后再进行下一个。  B、C、D均与原文相反。
全文开篇问“How can willpower be improved?” 随后给出各种方法：避免诱惑、利用储备、像肌肉一样锻炼、一次一个目标等。结尾总结“you can train your willpower”。整体目的是提供改善和保持意志力的建议。选项B解释为何变弱（只是部分内容），选项C仅提避免诱惑（片面），D讨论自控差导致的问题（非重点）。

answers.correct: Small actions that are done regularly.
answers.solution:
Small actions that are done regularly.

详解：根据原文第一段第3句“Small actions that are done regularly are what create long-lasting changes.”，可知规律进行的小行动能带来长期改变，答案是原文直接信息。

answers.correct: Habits that are easy and enjoyable.
answers.solution:
Habits that are easy and enjoyable.

详解：根据原文第三段第3句“Habits need to be two things to stick to: easy and enjoyable.”，可知容易坚持的习惯需要满足“简单”和“令人愉快”两个条件，答案是原文直接信息。

answers.correct: Because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.
answers.solution:
Because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.

详解：根据原文第六段第2句“As a result, these changes are more likely to last because they come from doing the same things again and again, not from a short time of hard work.”，可知这些改变之所以可能持续，是因为它们来自反复进行的相同行为，而非短时间的努力，答案是原文直接信息。

I have a habit of taking a 10-minute walk every night. First, it is easy to stick to because it doesn’t take much time and I can walk in the park near my home. Second, I enjoy listening to light music while walking, which makes the activity relaxing and fun for me.

详解：本题是开放性试题，答案不唯一，但观点需基于文中提到的习惯特点，如“简单易做”、“令人愉快”、“规律进行”等，结合自身生活经历作答即可。

<u>I have some suggestions.</u>   First, we can set up sports clubs like basketball and swimming. They help us keep fit and learn teamwork. Second, an art club is a good idea. We can draw and make crafts, making school life more colorful. Third, as many students love music, we can set up music clubs. We can enjoy music and talk about our favourite music as well. These activities are not only fun but also useful for our growth.   <u>I hope my suggestions will be put into practice.</u>

详解：［第一步：审题立意］ 
确定文体：介绍性说明文，用一般现在时为主 
明确要点：校园课后活动提议及理由
确定人称：第一人称（I/we）
注意事项：运用所给提示词语
［第二步：构思布局］
三段式结构： 
开头段：总起句，引出建议
主体段：分别介绍校园课后活动提议及理由
结尾段：总结与倡议
［第三步：要点展开］
要点一：校园课后活动提议及理由
运动俱乐部：set up sports clubs like basketball and swimming/keep fit and learn teamwork/healthy
美术俱乐部：an art club is a good idea/draw and make crafts/making school life more colorful/skills
音乐俱乐部：as many students love music/music clubs/enjoy music/talk about our favourite music as well /sing songs
要点二：总结
总结：not only fun but also useful for our growth/meaningful/lively
题目②

[二选一备选题目，与上一题任选其一作答；本题不重复计分]
<u>I’d like to share my experience of improving myself.</u>       Last term, I was worried about my poor English. So I decided to work harder. I asked my English teacher for help. He advised me to listen to the English audio as much as possible. What’s more, I read English every morning and kept trying to speak more. Finally, I got a high score in the exam.       This success has made me confident. I learn that if we keep trying, we can become better. I believe I will do even better in the future.

详解：［第一步：审题立意］ 
确定文体：介绍性记叙文，用一般过去时为主 
明确要点：自己进步的经历、从中学到了什么
确定人称：第一人称（I/my）
注意事项：不得透露真实姓名和学校名称
［第二步：构思布局］
三段式结构： 
开头段：总起句，引出对自己经历的介绍
主体段：分别介绍自己进步的经历
结尾段：总结自己从中学到了什么，升华主题
［第三步：要点展开］
要点一：自己进步的经历
起因：was worried about my poor English/in bad condition
经过：decided to work harder/asked my English teacher for help/advised me to listen to the English audio/as much as possible/What’s more/read English every morning/kept trying to speak more
结果：Finally/got a high score in the exam/get better
要点二： 从中学到了什么
收获：made me confident/learned that if we keep trying/become better/do even better in the future/nothing is impossible
